title: microcos henrici subsp. acuta
intro: Microcos henrici subsp. acuta is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the "butterfly flower" or "painted leaf." It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian and Indian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as its ability to reduce fever and relieve digestive issues. The health benefits of Microcos henrici subsp. acuta include its potential to protect against oxidative stress, improve immune function, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and may also be used in traditional remedies to treat various health conditions.
